channel.title: Home on Critical Thinking & English Writing I
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Critical Thinking & English Writing I

item.title: Syllabus
item.description:
批判性思维与英语写作（一）

Critical Thinking and English Writing I

Instrutor: 张军
Contact: jzhang3@ahu.edu.cn
Semester Length: 18 weeks · 2 × 45 minutes/week
Credits: 2
Target Students: Second-year English majors
Language of Instruction: English (with Chinese scaffolding where useful)
Course Type: Required, foundational

📚 Materials

  • Course Website (https://zjpsycholin.github.io/thinkwrite1/)
  • Lecture slides
  • Handouts
  • Readings

Main reference text

  • 张在新等.《英语写作教程》. 北京：外语教学与研究出版社, 2011.

Other reference texts

  1. 张莲、杨鲁新、李莉文等.《大学思辨英语教程—写作 2、3》. 外研社, 2015.
  2. 丁往道等.《英语写作手册》. 外研社, 2003.
  3. John Langman. College Writing Skills with Readings. 外研社, 2014.

📘 Course Description

This course introduces foundational academic writing skills through two core genres: description and narration. Students learn how to write vivid, concrete, and organized descriptive essays, as well as coherent and meaningful narratives. Emphasis is placed on clarity, precision, sensory detail, organization, and revision.

item.title: Week 1: Introduction to Description
item.description:
Week 1: Introduction to Description

These notes are designed to help you prepare before class and review after class. Use them to check your understanding of key concepts.

Learning Outcomes

After completing Week 1, you should be able to:

  • Define descriptive writing and explain its purpose
  • Distinguish between concrete and abstract language
  • Identify sensory details in sample texts
  • Write a short descriptive passage using concrete language
  • Understand the concept of “dominant impression”

Use this checklist to track your progress.

item.title: Week 2: Show, Don't Tell
item.description:
Week 2: Show, Don’t Tell

Learning Outcomes

After completing Week 2, you should be able to:

  • Distinguish between “telling” and “showing” in writing
  • Identify telling statements in your own and others’ writing
  • Transform abstract emotional statements into concrete actions and details
  • Convey character emotions through physical description, behavior, and dialogue
  • Apply showing techniques to create more engaging descriptive passages

Use this checklist to track your progress.

item.title: Week 5: Place Description
item.description:
Week 5: Place Description

Learning Outcomes

After completing Week 5, you should be able to:

  • Define “place” in writing and explain its importance in descriptive essays
  • Use spatial organization to guide readers through a location
  • Create a specific atmosphere through carefully selected sensory details
  • Establish a clear dominant impression in a place description
  • Connect a place to the person described in Essay 1
  • Continue drafting Essay 1 with attention to place

Use this checklist to track your progress.

item.title: Week 6: Organization & Coherence
item.description:
Week 6: Organization & Coherence

Learning Outcomes

After completing Week 6, you should be able to:

  • Identify and apply different organizational patterns in descriptive writing
  • Write effective topic sentences that establish focus for paragraphs
  • Create paragraph unity by ensuring every sentence supports a single main idea
  • Use transitional words and phrases to create smooth flow between sentences and paragraphs
  • Evaluate peer writing using structured feedback protocols
  • Revise your own essay for improved organization and coherence

Use this checklist to track your progress.

item.title: Week 3: Sensory Imagery & Precision
item.description:
Week 3: Sensory Imagery & Precision

Learning Outcomes

After completing Week 3, you should be able to:

  • Identify and use all five senses in descriptive writing
  • Choose precise nouns and powerful verbs over generic ones
  • Recognize and create effective similes, metaphors, and personification
  • Combine sensory details to create immersive experiences
  • Write a focused sensory paragraph with a dominant impression

Use this checklist to track your progress.

item.title: Week 4: Person Description
item.description:
Week 4: Person Description

Learning Outcomes

After completing Week 4, you should be able to:

  • Describe a person’s physical appearance using precise, concrete language
  • Reveal character indirectly through actions, habits, and behavior
  • Use clothing, posture, and gestures to suggest personality
  • Select meaningful details that create a dominant impression of a person
  • Begin drafting Essay 1: A descriptive portrait of a significant person

Use this checklist to track your progress.
